---
title: "Figure 4 — Validity map: log-det of the information distortion over Δ/τ × N_ch"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
PUBLICATION figure 4 = validity map (the "where is each approximation reliable?" map).

One scalar per grid cell: log det of the total information distortion matrix (IDM),
read straight from component_path = Probit_statistics_log_Det_Likelihood_Information_Distortion
(operation = probit.log_det, statistic = value), median = quantile_0.5 of the bootstrap.

  log_det(IDM) = 0   -> exact (Fisher == sample-score covariance, in volume)
  log_det(IDM) > 0   -> OVER-confident (Gaussian Fisher larger than the truth)
  log_det(IDM) < 0   -> under-confident

Axes per panel: x = N_ch (channels), y = Δ/τ_min (interval). Diverging fill centred at 0.

SCALES (per Luciano): three regimes have incomparable magnitudes, so each gets its own
colour scale instead of one global scale that would wash out R and IR:
  main figure  -> R, MR (shared signed scale)  +  IR (own signed scale, ~null everywhere)
  supplement   -> NR, NMR (own scale, distortion is an order of magnitude larger)

A cell is marked with a hollow dot when its 95% bootstrap interval (q0.025..q0.975)
INCLUDES 0, i.e. it is statistically indistinguishable from the exact likelihood.
That turns the heatmap into a validity map: dotted cells = "safe to use here".

Since N_sim = 10000 is fixed across the whole grid, the Jensen/convexity bias of log_det
is a roughly constant offset and does not move the spatial pattern; the debiased (Wald)
magnitude is only needed for an absolute "is it significant" call, handled here by the CI dot.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
```

```{r params}
DATA_DIR <- "data/433ed13"
NSIM     <- 10000
NOISES   <- c("0.1", "1")     # two measurement-noise conditions; noise=1 still missing for NR/NMR
NCH      <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 10000)   # IR is dense; others use the sparse subset
ALGOS    <- c("macro_NR","macro_NMR","macro_R","macro_MR","macro_IR")
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_NR="NR", macro_NMR="NMR", macro_R="R", macro_MR="MR", macro_IR="IR")

CP_LOGDET <- "Probit_statistics_log_Det_Likelihood_Information_Distortion"
noise_lab <- function(x) paste0("noise ", x)
```

```{r read}
grid  <- expand.grid(nch = NCH, algo = ALGOS, noise = NOISES, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
grid$path <- file.path(DATA_DIR, sprintf("figure_3_nch_%d_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s_battery_pool.csv",
                                         grid$nch, NSIM, grid$algo, grid$noise))
grid <- grid[file.exists(grid$path), ]

read_logdet <- function(p, noi) {
  read.csv(p, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    filter(component_path == CP_LOGDET,
           operation == "probit.log_det",
           statistic == "value") %>%
    select(algorithm, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, quantile_level, value) %>%
    mutate(noise = noi)
}

h <- map2_dfr(grid$path, grid$noise, read_logdet) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = quantile_level, values_from = value,
              names_prefix = "q") %>%
  transmute(
    algo  = factor(ALGO_LAB[algorithm], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)),
    noise = factor(noise, levels = NOISES),
    Num_ch, interval_in_tau,
    med   = q0.5, lo16 = q0.16, hi84 = q0.84, lo025 = q0.025, hi975 = q0.975,
    null_ok = (lo025 <= 0 & hi975 >= 0)          # 95% CI includes 0 -> indistinguishable from exact
  ) %>%
  mutate(Num_ch = factor(Num_ch, levels = sort(unique(Num_ch))),
         intv   = factor(interval_in_tau, levels = sort(unique(interval_in_tau))))
```

```{r plot-builder}
# one heatmap panel-group (noise rows x algo cols) with its own signed colour scale.
# axes: x = Δ/τ (interval, horizontal), y = N_ch (channels, vertical)
heat <- function(df, limit, title, show_legend = TRUE) {
  ggplot(df, aes(intv, Num_ch, fill = med)) +
    geom_tile(colour = "grey85", linewidth = .3) +
    geom_point(data = ~ filter(.x, null_ok), shape = 21, size = 1.6,
               colour = "grey20", fill = NA, stroke = .5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", med)), size = 2.2, colour = "grey15") +
    facet_grid(noise ~ algo, labeller = labeller(noise = noise_lab)) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166AC", mid = "white", high = "#B2182B",
                         midpoint = 0, limits = c(-limit, limit),
                         oob = squish, name = "log det(IDM)") +
    labs(x = expression(Delta/tau[min]),
         y = expression(N[ch]~"(channels)"), title = title) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
          legend.position = if (show_legend) "right" else "none",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"))
}
```

```{r fig4-main, fig.width = 11, fig.height = 7}
L_RMR <- ceiling(max(abs(h$med[h$algo %in% c("R","MR")]), na.rm = TRUE) * 10) / 10
L_IR  <- 0.5   # IR structure lives in +/-0.25; the one noisy N_ch=10 cell saturates

p_rmr <- heat(filter(h, algo %in% c("R","MR")), L_RMR,
              "R / MR (shared signed scale)")
p_ir  <- heat(filter(h, algo == "IR"), L_IR,
              "IR (own scale; flat at null; dots = indistinguishable from exact)")

g4 <- p_rmr / p_ir +
  plot_annotation(title = "Figure 4. Validity map: log det of the information distortion over interval and N_ch",
                  subtitle = "0 = exact, >0 over-confident, <0 under-confident, dot = 95% CI includes 0")
g4
ggsave("Figure_4_heatmap.pdf", g4, width = 11, height = 7)
```

```{r fig4-supp, fig.width = 9, fig.height = 4}
L_SUP <- ceiling(max(h$med[h$algo %in% c("NR","NMR")], na.rm = TRUE))
p_sup <- ggplot(filter(h, algo %in% c("NR","NMR")), aes(intv, Num_ch, fill = med)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "grey85", linewidth = .3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", med)), size = 2.6, colour = "grey15") +
  facet_wrap(~ algo, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#67001F", limits = c(0, L_SUP),
                      oob = squish, name = "log det(IDM)") +
  labs(x = expression(Delta/tau[min]), y = expression(N[ch]~"(channels)"),
       title = "Figure 4 supplement. Non-recursive NR, NMR (noise 0.1; noise 1 pending)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"))
p_sup
ggsave("Figure_4_heatmap_supp_NR_NMR.pdf", p_sup, width = 9, height = 4)
```
